Lathraea palmata is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its yellowish flowers and unique growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.

The bioactive constituents of Lathraea palmata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These compounds may contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

The most common herbal preparations made with Lathraea palmata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and hypertension.
